title: 10 basic tools for freelance web design
body:
## Working as a *freelance* requires creating an appropriate environment to successfully carry out projects, dedicating the necessary time in an adequate and quiet physical space as far as possible from distractions (domestic, leisure, etc.)

**In this *post* I reflect my 10 fundamental tools for the development of my tasks, they are not all nor are they the best, they are simply the ones I feel most comfortable with.** The essential thing is a ***hardware***, in my case a light but powerful **laptop**, with a good screen (16", 1600x900px) and dedicated graphics card. I chose a laptop because if needed you can transport it to the client's home, meetings or work occasionally outside the office. For more comfort in the office I have an extra monitor (23", 1920x1080) and a mouse and a more ergonomic full keyboard, as well as a printer that I use less and less. To stay permanently connected and even carry out some tasks (check and reply to emails, tweet, etc) a **mobile phone with data plan** has become essential, in my case an **Android** HTC. I also have a small ***netbook*** with Jolycloud OS that due to its lightness (less than 1kg) I transport to events or meetings where the mobile falls short and the laptop is heavy. Although in this Apple OSX world the OS is the most used Operating System, on the office laptop I currently use Windows 7. The basic applications can be divided into 2 types, the ***desktop*** *(which you install locally on your computer)* and the ***online*** (applications and utilities that live and run on the internet, "the cloud"). For me the ideal would be that all were in the **cloud**, so you wouldn't depend on a single specific *hardware* but could access them from any device. There are online graphics and programming applications, but they are not yet powerful and manageable enough to develop a project with agility. Currently out of the 10 applications I use most I only have 3 installed on my computer (the browser, the graphics editor and the web editor)

1.  ***Web* browser**: It is the queen application, always open, in addition to searching for information it is the bridge to use all online applications, tools and utilities, as well as, thanks to its *[plugins](http://www.xarop.com/tag/plugins/)*, help me perform some basic tasks in web development (inspect and adjust codes, capture screenshots, etc.). I currently use **Chrome** for its speed and simplicity although I also have Firefox, Safari, Explorer and Opera installed to test the sites I develop.
2.  **Graphics editor**: The [designs](http://www.xarop.com/tag/diseno/), after planning them in writing and sketching them on paper must be captured in image. In this case Adobe's **Fireworks** is my second most used software as it is specifically designed for this purpose (web design). I occasionally use Photoshop for some photo retouching, and Flash and some video editor for animations.
3.  **Web editor**: To layout and "write code" (HTML, CSS, PHP, js, ...) I have been using **Dreamweaver** for many years, I also use it as a file manager and FTP client for my sites.
4.  **Email**: Email is fundamental, and being able to have it from any device (mobile, netbook, tablet, etc) and from anywhere is vital, so I have chosen **Gmail** from Google, it is fast and powerful and in it I have [all my email accounts](http://www.xarop.com/todos-tus-emails-en-gmail/). From it I use the instant messaging that the Gtalk platform offers integrated and without leaving the web.
5.  **Office suite**: Microsoft Office is still the most used suite, I have replaced it locally with Open Office (open and free), but basically I use **Google Docs** to make budgets, invoices, presentations, etc. since these documents stay in the cloud and I can view and edit them from anywhere.
6.  **Storage/backups**: Having a backup copy of your files, syncing them from various computers and at the same time being able to access them from anywhere has made **Dropbox** an essential tool.
7.  **Social Networks**: Staying up to date, seeing what colleagues are doing, observing trends, sharing knowledge, etc. etc. make social networks a medium that no professional can ignore. On **[LinkedIn](http://es.linkedin.com/in/xarop)** I have my CV and my most professional contacts (potential clients), **[twitter](http://www.twitter.com/xarop)** is essential for exchanging *feedback* with colleagues in the field and **[facebook](http://www.facebook.com/xarop)** with the world in general.
8.  **CMS**: To build and, later, manage a website, the best alternative is to use a content manager (Content Manager System) **[WordPress](http://www.xarop.com/tag/wordpress/)** is free, simple and powerful thanks to the number of developers and *plugins* that this platform has.
9.  **RSS**: There are many interesting *blogs*, I always dedicate a moment of the day to check new *posts* in the feed aggregator, in my case **Google Reader**.
10.  **Task manager**: Managing tasks is essential for good productivity, especially if you work as a team.  **Hiveminder** is a good *online* platform for this purpose.

Then in my *bookmarks* I have countless links to tools for more specific things and a multitude of icon libraries, fonts, images, templates, tutorials, etc. **What are your essential tools?**
